author: Liam Darmody
commentText:
A structural reality most overlook:

SaaS economics rely heavily on human constraints—per-seat pricing assumes human operators. When the operator is a script running continuously, the pricing model collapses.

The vendors who recognize this early will survive the transition.
https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Steven Joshua Taylor
commentText:
Accountability remains the unresolved tension here.

When a system executes a decision autonomously, the liability does not disappear—it just shifts.

We are building the technical capacity faster than the legal frameworks required to govern it.
https://veda.ng/universal-text-ui

author: Parth Kapadia
commentText:
Implementation is fundamentally an operations problem, not an engineering one.

If you do not map the entire organizational logic before deploying the system, you are just moving the friction from one department to another.

https://veda.ng/ai-implementation-playbook

author: Stephanie Barnett
commentText:
Governments have a surprising advantage here.

Those that invested early in robust digital identity primitives—like Estonia or Singapore—can deploy these autonomous systems with immediate cryptographic trust.

Identity is the prerequisite for autonomy.
https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Steven Yurisich
commentText:
The bottleneck is rarely compute.

It is institutional inertia. Organizations spend millions on infrastructure, yet refuse to redesign the underlying workflows that made them slow in the first place.

You cannot automate a broken process and expect velocity.
https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Josh Bersin
commentText:
The true cost of these systems is rarely in the deployment.

It is in the maintenance of the orchestration layer. Edge cases compound exponentially when systems interact with the physical world.

Robust error handling is where the actual engineering happens.
https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Sutherland Global
commentText:
We are approaching a point where the marginal cost of intelligence trends toward zero.

The implications for software engineering are obvious. The implications for macroeconomic structure and labor markets are barely being discussed.

https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Alexander Whedon
commentText:
The obsession with general-purpose systems is a distraction.

Real economic value is being captured by teams building narrow, highly deterministic tools for specific vertical problems.

Precision scales better than general competence.
https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai

author: Ankur Bhatt
commentText:
We built the internet for human consumption.

Now we are forcing machine-readable systems to parse human-centric interfaces. The next foundational shift is not better parsing—it is native infrastructure built exclusively for algorithmic access.

https://veda.ng/stepwise-ai
